grantTitle: Pilot access schemes to Technology Infrastructures for European startups, scaleups and innovative SMEs
programName: HORIZON-CL4-2026-01 — INDUSTRY (Cluster 4: Digital, Industry and Space)
summary: This Coordination and Support Action aims to develop and test pilot multi-site access schemes that enable startups, scaleups, and innovative SMEs to access Technology Infrastructures for testing, upscaling, and validating new products and technologies. The action will establish a centrally managed and funded access programme with simplified and standardised conditions across the EU, focusing on priority areas including advanced materials, clean energy, and health/biotechnology.
objectives: Develop a sound understanding of specific needs of industrial users (innovative SMEs, startups, scaleups) for Technology Infrastructure services in priority areas
Increase opportunities for industrial users to develop, test, upscale and deploy new technologies via Technology Infrastructure services
Strengthen innovation and technology development capacity of European SMEs, startups and scaleups through improved access to Technology Infrastructures
Enhance attractiveness and effectiveness of access schemes to broaden the range of Technology Infrastructure users, especially startups and scaleups
Establish a pilot centrally managed and funded access programme with simplified and standardised access conditions applicable across the EU
Enhance transnational and multisite collaboration among Technology Infrastructures to foster ecosystem building

requirements.description: Applicants must be from countries described in Annex B of the Work Programme General Annexes (EU Member States and Associated Countries)
requirements.evidence: Legal entity validation and establishment in eligible country

requirements.description: Single-stage submission process with proposal page limits and layout as described in Part B of the Application Form
requirements.evidence: Completed application form within specified page limits

requirements.description: Financial and operational capacity requirements as described in Annex C of the Work Programme General Annexes
Must involve a critical mass of Technology Infrastructure operators and users in priority areas (advanced materials, clean energy, health/biotechnology)
requirements.evidence: Partnership agreements and letters of commitment from Technology Infrastructure operators

requirements.description: Should build on existing initiatives with already developed single access points (e.g., Open Innovation Test Beds or integrated networks)
requirements.evidence: Documentation of existing initiatives and access mechanisms

requirements.description: Must coordinate with the envisaged European Charter of access for industrial users to research and technology infrastructures
requirements.evidence: Alignment plan with European Charter principles

requirements.description: Must include development and implementation of visibility and promotion activities to demonstrate uptake across the EU
Should aim at significant broadening of user base to address needs of startups and scaleups without local access to such facilities
requirements.evidence: Strategy for user base expansion and target metrics

evaluationCriteria.name: Excellence
evaluationCriteria.description: Quality and credibility of the research; level of innovation; appropriateness of methodology and approach
keyIndicators: Understanding of specific needs of industrial users
Quality of proposed access scheme design
Innovation in approach to simplifying and standardising access
Building on existing initiatives and infrastructure networks

evaluationCriteria.name: Impact
evaluationCriteria.description: Credibility of the pathways to achieve expected outcomes and impacts; suitability and quality of the measures to maximize expected outcomes and impacts
keyIndicators: Increased opportunities for technology development and deployment
Strengthened capacity of European SMEs, startups and scaleups
Effectiveness in broadening Technology Infrastructure user base
Quality of communication and dissemination strategy
Plans for transnational access and collaboration

evaluationCriteria.name: Quality and efficiency of implementation
evaluationCriteria.description: Quality and effectiveness of the work plan; appropriateness of the management structures and procedures; quality and relevant experience of the individual participants
keyIndicators: Quality of work plan for pilot access programme implementation
Appropriateness of consortium composition and management structures
Capacity to coordinate multiple Technology Infrastructure operators

categories.description: Eligible costs will take the form of a lump sum as defined in the Decision of 7 July 2021
rules: Lump sum approach as per EU decision authorising use under Horizon Europe
Detailed budget table required (HE LS format)
Costs calculated according to lump sum methodology

categories.description: Centrally managed and funded access programme for SMEs, startups and scaleups
rules: Must provide simplified and standardised access conditions
Should allow quick access to needed services
May include financial support to third parties (access beneficiaries)

timeline.duration: Approximately 5 months (as per standard Horizon Europe timeline)
To be specified in proposal (typically 3-4 years for CSA actions)
deliverables: Pilot access scheme development and testing

timeline.duration: Up to 4 years after action end
deliverables: Granting authority may object to transfer of ownership or exclusive licensing of results

targetBeneficiaries: Scaleups requiring testing and validation facilities
Innovative SMEs needing access to state-of-the-art research and technology facilities
Technology Infrastructure operators seeking to broaden user base
Industrial users in advanced materials sector
Industrial users in clean energy sector
Industrial users in health and biotechnology sector
Companies without access to technology facilities in their local ecosystems
eligibleCountries: Certain non-EU/non-Associated Countries with specific funding provisions (see Horizon Europe Programme Guide)
keyThemes: Strategic autonomy in raw materials and innovative materials
additionalNotes: This is a Coordination and Support Action (CSA), not a Research and Innovation Action
Expected to fund 2 grants from a total budget of 5 million EUR
Individual grant size ranges from 1.5 to 2.5 million EUR
Proposals should coordinate with the envisaged European Charter of access for industrial users
Action should build on existing initiatives like Open Innovation Test Beds or other integrated networks
Lump sum funding model applies (not traditional cost reimbursement)
Financial support to third parties may be included for access programme beneficiaries
Emphasis on simplifying complex access mechanisms and reducing costs for growing innovative companies
Communication and dissemination are critical components to increase visibility and demonstrate uptake
Proposals may benefit from sharing best practices with other Technology Infrastructure projects
The action contributes to the destination: 'Achieving technological leadership for Europe's open strategic autonomy in raw materials, chemicals and innovative materials (2026-27)'
Standard Horizon Europe eligibility, admissibility, and evaluation procedures apply as per General Annexes
Model Grant Agreement: Lump Sum MGA applies
Applicants must use Standard application form (HE CSA) available in Submission System
